title: You don’t need a work permit - Canada.ca
content:
## You don’t need a work permit
To work in Canada as a news reporter or as part of a foreign film or media crew
FIFA-invited workers and volunteers
You don’t need a work permit to work in Canada if you’ve been directly invited to work at the FIFA World Cup 26™.
You may need to apply for a visitor visa or an electronic travel authorization (eTA) .
- media and broadcast personnel working at a FIFA event You may still need an eTA or visa to come to Canada. You should flag FIFA in your application .
- You may still need an eTA or visa to come to Canada. You should flag FIFA in your application .
- a news reporter or member of a reporter’s crew
- a member of a film or media crew who will not enter the Canadian labour market
- a journalist who works for a print, broadcast or Internet news service (journal, newspaper, magazine, television show, etc.) and your company is not Canadian
- a resident correspondent
- a manager or member of clerical staff, as long as the event is short term (6 months or less)
If this describes you, you don’t need a work permit.
If you’re coming to Canada to work for a Canadian media outlet , you need a work permit with a labour market impact assessment .

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
